commands.review_summary:
The branch updates OAuth token refresh persistence to merge observed granted scopes into stored token metadata, add a service label only when the observed grant covers that service, and cover scope-upgrade and partial-grant cases in tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows `auth list` reads stored token metadata while the refresh persistence path does not reconcile observed scopes; I did not run a live OAuth account in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; this collaborator-authored auth PR should proceed through normal maintainer review and CI.

target.title: fix(auth): reconcile observed OAuth scopes
target.body:
## Summary
- Reconcile stored OAuth scope metadata from scopes observed in successful token refresh responses.
- Add the service label only when the observed grant covers that service's canonical scope set.
- Add regression coverage for observed scope upgrades and partial-scope service over-reporting.

Fixes #649.

## Testing
- `go test ./internal/googleapi -run 'TestPersistingTokenSource|TestTokenSourceForAccountScopes'`
- `go test ./internal/googleapi ./internal/googleauth ./internal/cmd`
- `make test`
- Live clawdbot smoke: `gmail labels list` returned 16 labels; post-refresh `auth list` reported Gmail service and 47 scopes.
- Autoreview: clean.
target.author: steipete
